description_long: This object is an elongated, pointed stone with a rugged, irregular surface and a dark, earthy coloration. Its form tapers to a sharp tip at one end, while the opposite end is broader and more blunt, giving it a distinctly asymmetrical profile. The surface is marked by natural pitting and roughness, with subtle variations in color ranging from deep brown to muted gray, and some lighter patches. There are visible signs of weathering, including minor abrasions and a matte finish, suggesting exposure to the elements. On one side, there are white markings or inscriptions, possibly catalog numbers, which stand out against the darker stone. The overall appearance is that of a naturally formed or minimally worked stone, suitable for use as an obstacle or barrier in an urban environment.
description: A dark, pointed stone with a rough, irregular surface and visible weathering, featuring white markings on one side.
description_view_0: A narrow, edge-on view showing the stone's thin profile and pointed tip.
description_view_1: A side view highlighting the elongated, tapered shape and rough surface texture.
description_view_2: Another edge-on view, similar to view 0, emphasizing the stone's slimness and irregular outline.
description_view_3: A broad side view displaying the full length, surface roughness, and visible white markings.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or obstacle on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottling and pitting with some lighter patches and white painted markings; no regular or artificial patterns.
attribute_weathering: Rough and matte with visible abrasions and minor surface pitting, indicating moderate weathering from outdoor exposure.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved or manicured urban surfaces but may blend with natural landscaping elements such as soil or mulch.
